name: CadQuery (Parametric 3D CAD in Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); CadQuery community / David Cowden

performance.notes: OpenCASCADE kernel via OCP; complex models can be slow

agent_readiness.notes: Parametric 3D CAD modeler scripted in Python using OpenCASCADE. import cadquery as cq. result = cq.Workplane('XY').box(10, 10, 5).faces('>Z').workplane().hole(3). result.val().exportStep('out.step'). result.val().exportStl('out.stl'). Fluent API: .rect(), .circle(), .extrude(), .revolve(), .loft(), .shell(), .fillet(), .chamfer(). Selectors: faces('>Z'), edges('|Z'), vertices('<XY'). Assembly: cq.Assembly(); assy.add(part, loc=cq.Location((x,y,z))). Export: STEP, STL, DXF, SVG, GLTF. CQ-editor GUI available. Jupyter: cq.occ_impl.geom.Vector.

gotchas: OpenCASCADE kernel — complex boolean ops can fail silently or produce invalid geometry
Selector syntax ('>Z', '|X') is powerful but takes time to learn
Assembly constraints are limited compared to FreeCAD or SolidWorks
Large assemblies with many parts can be slow
best_when: Parametric CAD models scripted in Python (parts that change with parameters)
Generating STEP/STL files programmatically for 3D printing or manufacturing
Design automation and generative part families
When FreeCAD GUI is too complex and you want code-first CAD
avoid_when: Large assembly constraint systems (use FreeCAD Assembly4 or SolidWorks)
FEA mesh generation (use gmsh or Netgen)
summary: CadQuery is a code-first parametric 3D CAD library using OpenCASCADE. Fluent API: cq.Workplane('XY').box(10,10,5).hole(3). Selectors for faces/edges/vertices. .extrude(), .revolve(), .loft(), .fillet(). Export STEP, STL, DXF. Assembly support. Apache-2.0. Best for programmatic parametric parts and design automation. Selector syntax is a learning curve. Silent geometry failures possible with complex booleans.
